about.title: About Project NEXUS
about.summary: Project NEXUS is an open-source multi-tenant platform for community workflows, timebanking, and mutual support.
about.section1Body: The platform helps members share time, skills, opportunities, resources, events, messages, groups, and community marketplace activity.
about.section2Body: Project NEXUS is released under the AGPL-3.0-or-later license with attribution and source-code availability requirements.
about.section3Body: The mobile app focuses on member workflows. Administrative, broker, and developer operations remain on the web where larger screens fit the work better.

contact.title: Contact and support
contact.summary: Use the support routes that match your question, account issue, or tenant/community request.
contact.section1Body: For sign-in, profile, notification, privacy, or safety issues, include enough detail for the support team to identify the affected account and tenant.
contact.section2Body: Community organisers should use the canonical contact page for tenant-level questions, feature requests, and operational support.
contact.section3Body: If a concern involves immediate harm or unlawful activity, contact the relevant local emergency or legal authority first, then report it through the platform.

terms.title: Terms of use summary
terms.summary: These points summarise expected platform use. The web terms remain the canonical legal text.
terms.section1Body: Members are expected to provide accurate information, respect other users, and use timebanking, marketplace, messaging, and group tools lawfully.
terms.section2Body: Tenant and group rules may add workflow-specific expectations, especially for exchanges, volunteering, events, safeguarding, and marketplace activity.
terms.section3Body: Open the web version for the full, current terms, including legal notices, attribution, liability, and licence information.

privacy.title: Privacy summary
privacy.summary: Project NEXUS processes account, community, workflow, and security data to provide the service.
privacy.section1Body: Profile details, messages, listings, events, applications, transactions, and settings are used to operate the features you choose to use.
privacy.section2Body: Mobile settings include privacy, notification, blocked-user, translation, and data export workflows where supported by the account and tenant.
privacy.section3Body: Open the web privacy policy for the complete legal basis, retention, processor, rights, and contact information.

cookies.title: Cookies and storage summary
cookies.summary: The mobile app uses local device storage and secure tokens rather than browser cookies for most signed-in workflows.
cookies.section1Body: The app may store authentication state, preferences, cached API data, theme choices, and language settings on the device.
cookies.section2Body: When you open canonical web pages, browser cookies and similar technologies may apply according to the web cookie policy.
cookies.section3Body: You can sign out, adjust settings, clear app storage through your device, or open the web cookie policy for the full current text.

accessibility.title: Accessibility summary
accessibility.summary: The platform aims to support accessible member workflows across web and mobile experiences.
accessibility.section1Body: Mobile screens use translated labels, accessible buttons, visible loading/error states, safe areas, and platform assistive technologies where practical.
accessibility.section2Body: An accessible HTML-first frontend exists for users who benefit from a simpler, highly structured web experience.
accessibility.section3Body: Report barriers through the contact route so accessibility issues can be triaged and fixed with enough device and workflow detail.

trust.title: Trust and safety summary
trust.summary: Trust and safety workflows help protect members while keeping community exchange practical and respectful.
trust.section1Body: Profiles, reviews, reports, blocks, identity checks, and moderation workflows support safer exchanges and communication.
trust.section2Body: Use report, block, or contact routes when content, messages, listings, events, or member behaviour appear unsafe or inappropriate.
trust.section3Body: Platform tools do not replace emergency services, legal advice, or safeguarding authorities when immediate or statutory action is needed.
